question: What sovereign compute capacity does Canada have for training frontier AI models as of 2025?

evidence_pool.span_text: ISED's Canadian Sovereign AI Compute Strategy commits $2B to domestic training-grade compute through 2027, targeting deployment of approximately 11,000 advanced GPUs across federally-supported facilities.
CIFAR Canada AI Chair retention rate sat at approximately 71% over 2018-2024, against an estimated 65-80% peer-country range.
Canada's per-capita sovereign-compute commitment in 2024 was approximately 25% of the US figure and 60% of the UK figure, normalised by population.

verified_sentences.sentence_text: Canada's 2024 Sovereign AI Compute Strategy commits $2B and targets ~11,000 advanced GPUs by 2027 [#ev:ev_ai_001:400-700]; per-capita this is roughly 25% of US and 60% of UK commitments [#ev:ev_ai_003:600-820].
CIFAR Canada AI Chair retention sits at ~71% over 2018-2024 [#ev:ev_ai_002:100-280].

frame_coverage.frame_name: AI talent retention vs US/UK brain drain
